message:
refactor(parser,pager,vfs,wal): housekeeping cleanups and WAL FEC receiver fix

Parser:
- Simplify `parse_expr_bp` by using the existing `with_recursion_guard` helper
  instead of manually calling `enter_recursion`/`leave_recursion`. This
  eliminates duplicate guard logic and ensures the recursion depth is always
  decremented, even on early returns.
- Widen `with_recursion_guard` visibility from private to `pub(crate)` so
  `expr.rs` can call it.

Pager:
- Reformat two `retained_lock_level_after_txn_exit` call sites where rustfmt
  now prefers a different line break position after surrounding code changes.

VFS (unix):
- Add `#[allow(clippy::unused_self, clippy::items_after_statements)]` to
  `InodeTable::shard_idx` to suppress lint warnings introduced by the recent
  inode lock table sharding refactor.

WAL:
- Checkpoint executor: reformat a long `HashMap` binding to satisfy rustfmt.
- WAL FEC repair pipeline: fix `drain_abandoned_work` to take `&mpsc::Receiver`
  instead of `&mut mpsc::Receiver`. The `try_recv` method only requires a
  shared reference, and the mutable borrow was unnecessarily restrictive,
  preventing the compiler from proving the receiver wasn't aliased during
  the shutdown sequence. Also scope the `_mask` guard in `shutdown()` so it
  drops before awaiting the worker join handle.

Btree:
- Remove trailing whitespace in index_seek_bug_test2.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
